metric_value: Three-tier: NC OS Substrate + NC Required System Services + NC Applications
notes: Can run on single host or distributed across multiple hosts

metric_value: DEC Unix, HP/UX 10, IBM AIX, Sun SPARC Solaris, Windows NT; plus low-cost NCI-branded substrate
notes: Hardware-specific but allows abstracted services layer

metric_value: BOOTP-based; provides IP address and software images to NC Clients
notes: Future: DHCP for flexible IP allocation

metric_value: DHCP -- more flexible IP allocation; important scalability for large deployments
notes: Planned for future version

metric_value: Smart card (NC Card) + password/PIN; higher security than magnetic stripe; enables VPN
notes: Initial: standard Unix file system security + single-function smart card

metric_value: Based on LDAP; models user/resource mappings; supports charging for resource access
notes: Not available in initial NC Server implementation

metric_value: Based on Sun NFS; centralized file storage (NC clients have no local storage)
notes: Drawback: insufficient security for VPNs; future: Web NFS + Oracle Universal Server

metric_value: Oracle Java-based suite 'Hatrick': word processor, spreadsheet, slide-show; integrates with Oracle InterOffice
notes: Optimized for diskless NCs with limited bandwidth

metric_value: Value-added implementation of Oracle Web Server; supports NC Required Services and NC Childsafe
notes: One of three primary channels for delivering information to NC

metric_value: Based on Oracle InterOffice; includes gateways to popular mail environments, POP3 support
notes: Three client interfaces: Inter Office Client, POP3 Client, Inter Office Web Connector

metric_value: Key values: low cost of operation, security, scalability to accommodate several thousands of users
notes: Open, flexible, cost-effective design

metric_value: NC Server will be backward compatible with NC Clients for maximum period of four years
notes: Interoperable with all NCs subscribing to NC Reference Profile (Open Group)

metric_value: unknown
notes: NCI and the NC initiative largely failed commercially

metric_value: Web-based management tools based on Oracle Enterprise Manager; single-transaction user provisioning
notes: Future: Java-based with remote async task execution, resource monitoring

metric_value: Low-cost centrally administered alternative to traditional PC infrastructure; targets SOHO, Education, Community, Enterprise
notes: Value: profile stays the same regardless of access location

metric_value: Integrates with Surfwatch web content filter; future: Oracle Context language processing for dynamic filtering
notes: Targeted at preventing children accessing inappropriate content

metric_value: Pay-per-use or license software via NC Application Manager; integrates with Oracle Enterprise Manager and NC Authorization
notes: Provides alternate channel for low-cost software distribution

metric_value: Integrates with Oracle Financial Application Suite for complete business support solution
notes: Tracks resource usage per user; creates automated provisioning pipeline

notes: Bold claim that the NC paradigm would succeed
